visual_description: The 'GENERAL NOTES' title and box are drawn starting too high, causing them to physically overlap the text for 'AADT' and 'Cross-Streets' at the bottom of the 'PROJECT INFORMATION' list. Additionally, depending on the number of sheets, the 'SHEET INDEX' text can overflow its box vertically and overlap with the General Notes box.

confirmed_issues.issue: Boxes overflow and overwritten text. The Project Info box text overlaps with General Notes.

visual_description: The page shows a 'TYPICAL APPLICATION — TA-10: LANE CLOSURE USING FLAGGERS' schematic. Visually confirming the engineer's feedback: 1) The 'WORK AREA' text in the center has a white background box that is too small, causing the 'W' and 'A' to spill over into the crosshatch lines. 2) The row of rectangular zone labels ('ADVANCE WARNING AREA', 'TRANSITION', etc.) at the top completely intersects the W20-series signs on the right side of the page, causing overlapping/unreadable text. 3) On the far right, below the road, the 'END ROAD WORK' (G20-2) sign is drawn as an orange diamond instead of its correct regulatory rectangular shape.

confirmed_issues.issue: The 'WORK AREA' white background box is too small because the text width is being measured before the correct font size is applied.
The zone label row is positioned at `roadY1 - 65`, which places it directly over the opposing warning signs which peak around `roadY1 - 200`. It needs to be moved higher up the page.
The END ROAD WORK sign (G20-2) is incorrectly drawn as an orange diamond rotated 45 degrees, rather than an orange rectangle with text contained inside.

visual_description: A 'SITE-SPECIFIC WORK ZONE LAYOUT' sheet with a central satellite map. Below the map on the right side, there is a bounding box titled 'INTERSECTIONS WITHIN WORK ZONE:'. Inside the box, a list of 6 cross-streets is displayed. Overlapping the 5th and 6th list items ('5. S Hamlin Ln...' and '6. Emmett Hwy...') is the gray disclaimer text 'Route polyline and markers provided by Google Maps Platform. Verify on-site before construction.', making both the list and the disclaimer difficult to read.

confirmed_issues.issue: The Google Maps disclaimer text has a static Y-coordinate that overlaps the dynamic cross-streets list when the list contains multiple items.

visual_description: A schematic of a 4-way intersection (SH-44 and W State St). There is a signal icon on the top right corner with a 'TURN LANE' label. The vertical road (W State St) has solid black stop bars drawn on its approaches to the intersection. The horizontal road (SH-44) does not have any stop bars. W20-1 'ROAD WORK AHEAD' signs are shown on the cross street (top and bottom approaches), and the notes table confirms 'W20-1 Qty: 2'.

confirmed_issues.issue: Missing stop bars on SH-44 when a signal is present at the intersection.

Only showing W20-1 signs on the cross-street. For a flagger operation at a signalized intersection (signal on flash), the full flagging series (W20-1, W20-4, W20-7a) should be displayed on all active approaches.

disputed_issues.issue: The intersection should be grounded in real data — look up how busy the cross street is, use Street View or satellite imagery to determine turn lanes, traffic controls, etc.
disputed_issues.reason: The AI system relies on OpenStreetMap (OSM) data and provided geospatial inputs at generation time; it cannot dynamically scrape Street View or live satellite feeds. Because data limitations exist, the plans are inherently marked 'PRELIMINARY — NOT FOR CONSTRUCTION' to mandate manual field verification by the engineer.

visual_description: The page is titled 'INTERSECTION DETAIL: W MOON VALLEY RD' and subtitled 'T-INTERSECTION (WEST)'. However, the drawing only shows the horizontal mainline road (SH-44) with no vertical or intersecting cross-street drawn. Additionally, the 'SIGNS REQUIRED AT THIS INTERSECTION' box incorrectly lists 'W20-1 ROAD WORK AHEAD — Qty: 0'.

confirmed_issues.issue: Cross-street geometry and signs fail to render for T-intersections due to case-sensitivity bug when parsing geo.type (e.g., 'T-West' instead of 'T-west')

disputed_issues.issue: Not showing the full sign sequence
disputed_issues.reason: The engineer is misinterpreting the blank schematic and Qty: 0 as a lack of sign data. Once the geometry type case sensitivity bug is fixed, the intersection leg will draw with the correct W20-1 sign placement, and the quantity will correctly update to 1. For a standard local T-intersection, a single W20-1 sign per approach meets the MUTCD baseline requirement, so a 'full sign sequence' (like those found on highways) is not expected here.

visual_description: The page displays the 'INTERSECTION DETAIL: S SHORT RD' with the subtitle 'T-INTERSECTION (WEST)'. However, the diagram shows a straight, continuous horizontal roadway (the mainline). The intersecting leg (which should branch out as a 'T') is completely missing. No masking or intersecting road edges are drawn.

confirmed_issues.issue: Intersection geometry missing for 'T-west' due to case-sensitivity bug. The variable `geo.type` likely holds a mixed-case string like 'T-West' or 'T-WEST', causing strict checks like `geo.type === 'T-west'` to fail, resulting in both `hasNorth` and `hasSouth` evaluating to false.

visual_description: The page titled 'INTERSECTION DETAIL: W SHULTZ CT' indicates the geometry is a 'T-INTERSECTION (WEST)'. However, the mainline roadway is drawn with completely solid, continuous edge lines and dashed centerlines. There is no cross-street geometry (no vertical lines, curb returns, or masks) drawn on either side of the main road.

confirmed_issues.issue: Intersection geometry fails to render because the geometry type check is case-sensitive. If the upstream data provides 'T-West' instead of exactly 'T-west', both hasNorth and hasSouth evaluate to false, causing no intersecting legs to be drawn.
confirmed_issues.fix:
<<<<
  // T-west/T-east map to north/south legs for the schematic (horizontal mainline)
  const hasNorth = geo.type === 'T-north' || geo.type === 'T-west' || geo.type === '4-way' || geo.type === 'Y' || !geo.type.startsWith('T-');
  const hasSouth = geo.type === 'T-south' || geo.type === 'T-east' || geo.type === '4-way' || geo.type === 'Y' || !geo.type.startsWith('T-');

  // Geometry type label
  const geoLabel = geo.type === '4-way' ? '4-WAY INTERSECTION' :
    geo.type.startsWith('T-') ? `T-INTERSECTION (${geo.type.replace('T-', '').toUpperCase()})` :
    geo.type === 'Y' ? 'Y-INTERSECTION' : geo.type === 'roundabout' ? 'ROUNDABOUT / CIRCULAR INTERSECTION' : 'INTERSECTION';
====
  // T-west/T-east map to north/south legs for the schematic (horizontal mainline)
  const gType = String(geo.type).toLowerCase();
  const hasNorth = gType === 't-north' || gType === 't-west' || gType === '4-way' || gType === 'y' || !gType.startsWith('t-');
  const hasSouth = gType === 't-south' || gType === 't-east' || gType === '4-way' || gType === 'y' || !gType.startsWith('t-');

  // Geometry type label
  const geoLabel = gType === '4-way' ? '4-WAY INTERSECTION' :
    gType.startsWith('t-') ? `T-INTERSECTION (${String(geo.type).replace(/^T-/i, '').toUpperCase()})` :
    gType === 'y' ? 'Y-INTERSECTION' : gType === 'roundabout' ? 'ROUNDABOUT / CIRCULAR INTERSECTION' : 'INTERSECTION';
>>>>

visual_description: The page displays the intersection detail for 'S HAMLIN LN', labeled as a 'T-INTERSECTION (WEST)'. The main road (SH-44) is drawn horizontally with solid edge lines and a dashed centerline, but the perpendicular cross-street lines and intersection geometry are completely missing. Only the continuous mainline is visible.

confirmed_issues.issue: Intersection geometry is completely missing due to case-sensitive strict equality checks on `geo.type`. When `geo.type` evaluates to an uppercase variant (e.g. 'T-WEST'), it fails the `geo.type === 'T-west'` check, resulting in both `hasNorth` and `hasSouth` evaluating to false, thus drawing no cross-street. It requires a normalized `.toLowerCase()` check.

The same case-sensitive `geo.type` bug exists when rendering cross-street stubs on the Geometry Plan, which causes cross-streets to vanish on those sheets as well. (Proactive fix to prevent identical failures downstream)

visual_description: The page shows a schematic of a 4-way intersection for 'Emmett Hwy' crossing the main road (SH-44). Only W20-1 'ROAD WORK AHEAD' signs are shown on the Emmett Hwy approaches, and the intersection is classified as a 'LOCAL ROAD INTERSECTION'. The 'SIGNS REQUIRED AT THIS INTERSECTION' box lists a quantity of 2 for W20-1 signs.

confirmed_issues.issue: Only showing W20-1 signs on cross-streets for flagging operations. If the main route uses flaggers, the cross-street approaches should also display the full flagger warning series (W20-1, W20-4, W20-7a).
The sign requirement quantity text does not reflect the addition of the full flagger sign series.
Emmett Hwy is being misclassified as a 'Local Road' rather than a Highway because the regex parser expects a route number (e.g., 'Hwy 16') and fails on unnumbered highway strings.

disputed_issues.issue: Should look up real intersection data.
disputed_issues.reason: The system intentionally generates standard *schematic* typical applications for intersections rather than exact CAD drawings of every real-world intersection radius and turn lane. This is industry-standard for TCPs. However, by fixing the 'isHighway' regex (addressed above), the system will now automatically flag 'Emmett Hwy' as a high-traffic highway and append the required enhanced measures and flagger notes to the schematic.

visual_description: Page 12 is an Index sheet showing the entire 2.5-mile route zoomed out. There is a massive cluster of overlapping orange signs, leader lines, text labels, and white boxes representing cross-street intersections crammed into the center of the page. Due to the zoomed-out scale of the index map, all the detailed annotations (which have a fixed pixel size) overlap each other, rendering the map completely illegible.

confirmed_issues.issue: Detailed annotations (signs, flaggers, cross-street labels, station ticks) are drawn on the zoomed-out Index sheet, causing massive overlapping and illegibility.
confirmed_issues.fix: Wrap the TCP devices and flagger annotations in an `if (!isIndexSheet)` block so they only render on the detailed geometry sheets.

confirmed_issues.issue: Station tick marks are drawn every 100 feet across a 13,000-foot route on a single index page, creating a solid bar of ink.
confirmed_issues.fix: Wrap the station tick rendering loop in an `if (!isIndexSheet)` block so they are suppressed on the overview index map.

confirmed_issues.issue: Cross-street labels and the main road label are rendered at fixed pixel sizes on the zoomed-out map, overlapping with grid tiles and each other in a massive cluster.
confirmed_issues.fix: Wrap both the main road label and the cross-street intersection label blocks in `if (!isIndexSheet)` to keep the index map clean and legible.

visual_description: The page displays the GEOMETRY PLAN for the first segment of the route. On the left, the primary approach warning signs are heavily clustered together, with all their leader lines pointing to the exact same black dot at the start of the alignment, causing overlapping and unreadable labels. Along the middle of the road, the blue cross-street label boxes are positioned directly on the road horizontally, their leader lines crisscross confusingly, and the text inside these boxes overlaps awkwardly with the sign diamonds.

confirmed_issues.issue: Primary and opposing approach signs are stacked at the same point because their stations are clamped to 0 or the end of the road. The work area calculation does not leave enough room for advance warning signs along the polyline.
Primary signs map to a hardcoded offset based on taperLengthFt rather than the newly calculated taperStartSta.
confirmed_issues.fix:
const visiblePrimary = ctx.blueprint.primary_approach
    .map(sign => ({ ...sign, sta: taperStartSta - sign.distance_ft }))
    .filter(s => s.sta >= viewportStartSta && s.sta <= viewportEndSta)
    .sort((a, b) => a.sta - b.sta);

confirmed_issues.issue: Opposing signs map to a hardcoded offset based on the end of the alignment and are clamped, causing them to stack.
confirmed_issues.fix:
const visibleOpposing = ctx.blueprint.opposing_approach
    .map(sign => ({ ...sign, sta: dnTaperEndSta + sign.distance_ft }))
    .filter(s => s.sta >= viewportStartSta && s.sta <= viewportEndSta)
    .sort((a, b) => a.sta - b.sta);

confirmed_issues.issue: Flagger symbols use the hardcoded taper lengths instead of the newly calculated station locations.

Cross-street callout boxes are incorrectly pushed horizontally along the road instead of vertically away from the road, causing them to render on top of the road surface and tangling their leader lines.
The sign code text inside the cross-street callout box is positioned exactly at the bottom tip of the sign diamond, creating a messy overlap.

visual_description: On page 14 (Geometry Plan), the main road has a continuous red work zone fill, but there are several clear visual artifacts. First, at the far right edge of the page, the text 'WORK AREA' is drawn with a white background mask that cuts a weird gap right through the road line. Second, at the 'EMMETT HWY' and 'S HAMLIN LN' intersections, the grey perpendicular cross-street lines do not actually connect to the main road; they start slightly outside the main road edge, leaving a noticeable 2-pixel gap. Finally, the black leader lines pointing to the cross-street sign boxes anchor directly to the center of the main road, slicing right through the red work zone instead of stopping at the edge of the road.

confirmed_issues.issue: The 'WORK AREA' label is placed at the global midpoint of the entire route rather than the midpoint of the visible viewport, causing it to fall on the edge of the page and create a weird white gap masking the road.
Cross-street line stubs leave a gap because they are programmed to start 2px outside the main road edge instead of connecting flush with the pavement.
South-facing cross-street line stubs also leave a 2px gap because they start outside the main road edge.
Intersection leader lines point to the exact centerline of the main road, visually cutting through the work zone area.

visual_description: The page displays the final segment of the route on the geometry plan. Multiple warning signs (W20-1, W20-4, W20-7a) and a FLAGGER symbol are clustered and overlapping at the exact same station at the road edge. Additionally, the black road edge lines and dashed centerline abruptly terminate and vanish midway across the page, leaving a gap where only the grey pavement fill is visible.

confirmed_issues.issue: Signs are all showing being at the same point
weird gaps in the road.
